name: core-network
title: GCP Core Network
summary: Creates a custom-mode global VPC with regional subnets (secondary ranges for GKE, per-subnet flow logs, Private Google Access), static routes, and optional Cloud NAT (router + NAT).

governance.name: prefix
governance.description: Short organization or project prefix. First token of every resource name: {prefix}-{cloud}-{environment}-{resource-type}-{suffix}.

governance.name: environment
governance.description: Deployment environment identifier (e.g., dev, test, prod). Used in resource names and mandatory labels.

governance.name: owner
governance.description: Team or individual that owns the deployed resources. Applied as the mandatory 'owner' label. GCP labels require lowercase letters, numbers, hyphens, underscores.

governance.name: cost_center
governance.description: Cost center code for chargeback/showback. Applied as the mandatory 'cost_center' label (lowercase).

governance.name: additional_tags
governance.description: Optional additional labels merged beneath the mandatory label set. GCP label keys/values must be lowercase. Mandatory labels win on key collision.

governance.name: name_suffix
governance.description: Final token of every resource name, used to disambiguate multiple instances of this module.

module.name: project_id
module.description: GCP project ID hosting the VPC. No hardcoded project IDs — always supplied by the consumer.

module.name: routing_mode
module.description: Dynamic routing mode for the VPC's Cloud Routers: REGIONAL or GLOBAL.

module.name: delete_default_routes_on_create
module.description: Delete the auto-created 0.0.0.0/0 route at VPC creation (strict egress posture; re-add explicit routes as needed).

module.name: subnets
module.type: map(object({ ip_cidr_range = string region = string private_ip_google_access = optional(bool, true) secondary_ip_ranges = optional(map(string), {}) flow_logs = optional(object({ enabled = bool aggregation_interval = optional(string, "INTERVAL_5_SEC") flow_sampling = optional(number, 0.5) metadata = optional(string, "INCLUDE_ALL_METADATA") }), { enabled = false }) }))
module.description: Map of subnets keyed by logical name. GCP subnets are REGIONAL (region per subnet, unlike AWS/Azure zonal-or-regional models). Flow logs are configured per subnet, not per network. secondary_ip_ranges supports GKE alias IPs.

module.name: static_routes
module.type: map(object({ dest_range = string priority = optional(number, 1000) next_hop_gateway = optional(string) next_hop_ip = optional(string) next_hop_ilb = optional(string) tags = optional(list(string), []) }))
module.description: Static routes keyed by logical name. Exactly one next_hop_* must be set per route.

module.name: cloud_nat
module.type: object({ enabled = bool region = optional(string) source_subnetwork_ip_ranges_to_nat = optional(string, "ALL_SUBNETWORKS_ALL_IP_RANGES") min_ports_per_vm = optional(number, 64) log_errors_only = optional(bool, true) })
module.description: Cloud NAT configuration. Creates a Cloud Router + NAT in the given region for private-subnet egress.

outputs.name: network_id
outputs.description: ID (self-link form) of the created VPC network.

outputs.name: network_name
outputs.description: Name of the VPC network (needed by firewall/peering/DNS modules).

outputs.name: network_cidr
outputs.description: GCP VPCs have no network-level CIDR (subnets carry ranges); always null. Present for cross-cloud output-contract parity.

outputs.name: subnet_ids
outputs.description: Map of logical subnet name to subnet ID.

outputs.name: subnet_self_links
outputs.description: Map of logical subnet name to subnet self-link.

outputs.name: subnet_cidrs
outputs.description: Map of logical subnet name to primary CIDR range.

outputs.name: route_ids
outputs.description: Map of logical static route name to route ID. (GCP has no route tables — routes attach to the network.)

outputs.name: nat_ids
outputs.description: Map with key 'nat' to the Cloud NAT ID. Empty when Cloud NAT is disabled.

outputs.name: flow_log_id
outputs.description: Always null — GCP flow logs are subnet-level settings, not standalone resources. Present for output-contract parity; see subnet log_config.
